steps.title: Fastest path: mark the givens first
steps.content: List the given angle relationships first: corresponding angles in congruent triangles, right angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are organized.

steps.title: Identify corresponding angles in the congruent triangles
steps.content: Since triangles PQR and XYZ are congruent with P ↔ X and Q ↔ Y, the correspondence is complete: P ↔ X, Q ↔ Y, and R ↔ Z. In triangle PQR, we know ∠P = 58° and ∠Q = 90°.

steps.title: Use angle sum to find angle Z
steps.content:
In triangle PQR, the sum of angles equals 180°:
$$58^{\circ} + 90^{\circ} + \angle R = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle R = 32^{\circ}$$
Since R corresponds to Z, ∠Z = 32°.
